methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series G → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.25x) to $75M total raised proxy.

data.narrative:
Earth AI is a Series G-stage infrastructure and logistics company founded by Roman Teslyuk that has raised $20M to date. No direct public or private comparables were available for this valuation exercise, limiting the ability to apply market multiples or precedent transaction analysis.

The valuation relied on stage-progression methodology, which applies a 1.25x step-up multiplier typical of Series G advancement dynamics to a $75M proxy base derived from total capital raised. This method yielded an implied value of $94M. A sensitivity range was constructed around this estimate by adjusting the multiplier assumption: a conservative 1.06x step-up produces a low bound of $80M, while a 1.44x multiplier yields a high bound of $108M.

The triangulated valuation range of $80M–$108M, with a median of $94M, reflects the single-method analysis framework. Key uncertainties include the absence of comparable company benchmarking, which would typically anchor valuations to revenue multiples or growth metrics within the infrastructure and logistics sector. The range's width is primarily sensitive to assumptions around stage-advancement velocity and investor demand at Series G. Additional validation through revenue-based or DCF methodologies would strengthen confidence in this estimate.
